meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve can't scale projects (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't scale projects. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Project Management for Students: Scale Your AI Apps
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with unscalable code. Learn the project management workflow for AI builders using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first app.
content.answer_block: Scaling issues stem from technical debt and disorganized architecture. By implementing a modular project management framework, you isolate logic, automate boilerplate, and maintain clarity. Use Antigravity to scaffold robust architectures and Claude Code to iterate rapidly, ensuring your project remains maintainable as you add complex features.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting your first AI application, but now the codebase feels like a fragile house of cards. Every new feature breaks three old ones, and the complexity is suffocating your progress. This isn't a failure of your coding ability; it is a failure of your project management architecture.

Great engineering isn't just about syntax; it's about structure. To transition from a student hacking together scripts to an AI Builder architect, you must adopt a modular, iterative workflow. We are going to restructure your chaos into a scalable pipeline using professional-grade tools that handle the heavy lifting for you.

steps.title: Define the Domain Model
steps.description: Before writing code, define your data schemas and entities to prevent future database bottlenecks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a domain model for a [Project Type] application. Focus on modularity and separation of concerns for the user, task, and AI interaction entities.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate schema.prisma from domain model'

steps.title: Establish the API Blueprint
steps.description: Standardize your endpoints to ensure the frontend and backend communicate without friction.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a clean RESTful API structure for my [Project Type] app, prioritizing stateless authentication and error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create API route handlers for defined endpoints'

steps.title: Implement Modular AI Services
steps.description: Decouple AI logic from your main controller so you can swap models without rewriting your app.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an abstract AI Service layer that supports multiple LLM providers with unified response interfaces.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Refactor AI logic into a standalone service directory'

steps.title: Automate Documentation and Testing
steps.description: Use automated documentation to keep track of your growing feature set and ensure stability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive README and a suite of unit tests for the current service layer modules.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate test files for all service modules'

steps.title: Continuous Integration Loop
steps.description: Set up a feedback loop where Claude Code reviews your changes against your project constraints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a custom linting and validation rule set for my project to prevent common scaling pitfalls.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Run code review on all recent commits'

pro_tips: Always build for the 'next' developer, even if that developer is just you in two weeks.
Use environment variables for all sensitive keys; never hardcode credentials, even in local testing.
Implement a 'feature flag' system early so you can toggle experimental features without breaking the main branch.
Write your documentation as you write your code; if it's not documented, it essentially doesn't exist.
Prioritize 'fail-fast' error logging to identify scaling bottlenecks before they crash your user experience.
content.next_steps: Once your architecture is stable, start deploying your app to a Vercel or Railway environment to test real-world latency and database scaling.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build software that lasts. Don't let your ideas get buried in spaghetti code—take control of your development lifecycle today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
